headlines.title: COOKED IN SAME FAMILY 50 YEARS
headlines.subtitle: Marry Grogan Celebrates Her Golden Anniversary As Kitchen's Head.
headlines.fullText: By Associated Press. NEW BRUNSWICK, N. J .. April 5. Mary Grogan resumed her duties an cook today. Yesterday she was the honored head of the family of Abial her was the fiftieth anniversary of employment, which in this day of household complexities and servant Problems was considered quen a mate feest ther made the day one of grateful celebration. Mary Grogan and friends of Friends of sian far an! near. the Prices came from fur an: ne de Heide orchestra of the vil-lage made muste while gifts and praises were showered on the old woman. From 4 until 7 a banquet was served, and Mary, with eyes dimmed with tears of joy, sat beside Mrs. Prire, Members of the family vied with each In paying her courtesies and cv: erybody made it a point to pass hier which lended the table. A cake adorned loaded hmm Mary and used In days gone by. Ity on April 5. 1555, and asked for $100 a year as wages. She entered the household under these terms and has never had a raise or a vacation since, and but very few days off. Al-though 82 years 6 4. Mary is still In good health and quedan Cone Prices, however, consider her one of the family and dearer than most rela-tives. .

headlines.title: CIRCULARS ATTACK LIGHT PLANT PLAN
headlines.subtitle: Call Attention to Election Almost Forgotten in Excitement.
headlines.fullText: Cireulars put about the city Monday attacking the electric light extension plans as a crippling of city funds when a greater water supply was needed re-called the fact that a city election will be held Tuesday, a matter that had al-most been forgotten In the excitement of the fire. Neither Mayor Harris nor Commis-sioner Mulkey agree with the etreular. Both point out that the $50,000 for light extension would be but a small amount with which to tackle the water supply question. Commissioner Mulkey, who will be a lead member of the Incoming commission also, as well as that which adopted the present expenditure budget eritfelsed. points out that it has been recommend-a ajuns that the water supply fund se cared for by band laque. He estimates that nearly a million mean at least a dogen of them on like light extension should not be wor. ed down when It would make available only $50,000. headlines.title: 34 ARRESTS MADE IN FIRE DISTRICT

headlines.title: Boil Your Water, Warning Issued; River Water In Use
headlines.fullText: River water was still being pumped Into the mains Monday morning with no time fixed for the return to all artesian. Bolling of water is there-fore advised for several days, as it will take some time to clear the reservoirs and mains of the river water even after By a remarkable piece plece of good for. for-tune, fire was already burning linder the boilers of the reserve pumping plant at the Junction of the West Fork under them, With the notice of the big fire they were quickly fired to full steam and 120 pounds of pressure put upon on the mains. So strong was the pressure secured that in a number of places huse linen were burst.
